title: spermatophyta
intro: The division Spermatophyta, also known as seed plants, is a taxonomic rank that encompasses plants that produce seeds. This division includes a wide range of plant species, from conifers to flowering plants. Examples of divisions within the Spermatophyta rank include Monocotyledonae, which comprises plants with a single cotyledon, such as grasses and lilies, and Magnoliopsida, a division of flowering plants that includes families like the rose and the apple tree. Another example is Polypodiopsida, a division that includes ferns.

classes.class_desc: Pinopsida, also known as the conifers, is a class of gymnospermous plants that includes trees and shrubs with needle-like leaves and cones.
Cycadopsida, a class of seed-producing plants that are typically evergreen, cone-bearing, and have a long history dating back to the Mesozoic era.
